description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The solar panels are elevated above a base structure that appears to be a concrete or masonry enclosure, possibly housing electrical or mechanical components. The frame is supported by angled metal struts, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight exposure. The base contains various geometric forms and compartments, some of which may be for maintenance access or equipment storage. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed structural elements and a focus on functionality. The installation is suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated solar farms.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal frame above a solid base, with visible support struts and compartmentalized sections below.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting frame, with the base structure and internal compartments visible beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled support struts and the depth of the base, with more detail visible in the compartmentalized sections below the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array, the supporting frame, and the internal structure of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled supports and the arrangement of compartments within the base, with a clear view of the panel's tilt.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: single large rectangular array, approximately 3x6 grid of panels
